headline: Tell us what kind of Georgia camp experience you want to plan
subheadline: Use this page for family package planning, group retreats, partnerships, and custom experience questions that need a real follow-up.
stepsHeadline: The contact route is designed to make follow-up easier and more informed

steps.title: Share the goal
steps.description: Tell us whether you are planning a family weekend, group retreat, partnership, or custom request.

steps.title: Route the inquiry
steps.description: The team can then decide whether the best next step is direct booking, planning support, or custom qualification.

steps.title: Move into the right path
steps.description: That keeps complex requests from being forced into the wrong checkout flow.

sections.title: Best for family planning
sections.description: Use this path when you want help choosing the right program or building a more personalized weekend.

sections.title: Best for groups
sections.description: Use this path for schools, churches, and organizations that need a custom retreat conversation.

sections.title: Best for partnerships
sections.description: Use this path for sponsorship, content, or brand collaboration opportunities.

leadForm.heading: Start the right planning conversation
leadForm.intro: Share enough detail to help us recommend the right path without sending you through the wrong form or booking flow.

faq.question: Should I use contact if I am ready to buy a direct package?
faq.answer: If the package fits your needs, pricing or the program page is faster. Contact is best when you want guidance or custom planning.

cta.heading: Explore pricing and programs if you want to compare options before reaching out.
cta.body: The site is built so you can either self-educate or start a conversation, depending on what you need.
